zh.Rolleston,Nottingham,England: 英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾洛勒斯頓
zh.lifeSummary: 當William DE NEVILLE MP於大約1336年出生在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾洛勒斯頓時，他的父親Thomas de Neville37歲，母親Cecily Blanchminster33歲。他於1359年與Elizabeth Fencotte在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾洛勒斯頓結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於大約1420年在他的家鄉享年86歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William DE NEVILLE MP于大约1336年出生在英国英格兰諾廷加姆爾洛勒斯頓时，他的父亲Thomas de Neville37岁，母亲Cecily Blanchminster33岁。他于1359年与Elizabeth Fencotte在英国英格兰諾廷加姆爾洛勒斯頓结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于大约1420年在他的家乡享年86岁。

nameMeanings.name: DE
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: NEVILLE
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Neuville in Calvados or Néville in Seine-Maritime, both so called from Old French neu(f) ‘new’ (from Latin novus) + ville ‘settlement’. One family line, originally from Neuville (Calvados), became powerful during the Wars of the Roses through Richard Neville (1428–71), 16th Earl of Warwick, nicknamed "The Kingmaker".
Irish (Munster): assimilation of the Gaelic name Ó Niadh (see Nee ) and sometimes of Ó Cnaimhín (see Nevin ).

History: George Neville came to VA in or c. 1700 and settled on the headwaters of the Occoquan River, acquiring a large estate. His descendants, bearing the surnames Neville and Craig, were of considerable importance in Pittsburgh, PA, and Cincinnati, OH.

variants.name: Nevells
meanings: Irish: variant of   Neville  .

variants.name: Nevils
meanings: English: variant of   Neville  .

variants.name: Novel
meanings: English: variant of   Neville  .
Catalan: variant of   Novell  .

variants.name: Newell
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Neville  .
English: variant of   Noel  .
English: variant of   Newhall  .

variants.name: Nevills
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of Neville , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Nevil
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Neville  .

variants.name: Newill
meanings: English and Irish (or Norman origin): variant of   Neville  .

variants.name: Nevill
meanings: English and Irish: of Norman origin, a variant of   Neville  .

variants.name: Nevels
meanings: English and Scottish (Wigtownshire): of Norman origin, a variant of Neville , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is now rare in Britain.
Dutch and North German: unexplained.

variants.name: Nee
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Dermot, John Patrick.

Irish (Galway): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Niadh ‘descendant of Nia’ or Mac Niadh ‘son of Nia’. Compare McNee . Needham 2 and Neville have also been adopted as Anglicized forms of this name.
North German: possibly a relationship name from Middle Low German neve ‘nephew, grand-child’.

nameMeanings.name: MP
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 米, which means ‘rice’: (i) from one of the ‘Nine Sogdian Surnames’, also known as ‘nine surnames of Zhaowu’, because their ancestors came from Zhaowu, an ancient city in present-day Gansu province in northwestern China. During the Sui and Tang dynasties (581–987  AD ) there were nine Sogdian states in Central Asia, one of which was called Mi (米) in Chinese (located in present-day Uzbekistan). Between 649 and 655  AD , these states submitted to the Emperor Gaozong of Tang (628–683  AD ). Immigrants from the state of Mi to China acquired the surname 米. (ii) for some families, the surname is borne by descendants of Mi Fu (米芾), an artist in the Northern Song dynasty (960–1127  AD ), who claimed he was a descendant from the royal families of the state of Chu in the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) and changed his original surname 芈 to 米, a different Chinese character with the same pronunciation.

Mandarin form of the surname 宓 (also pronounced as Fu in Mandarin): (i) said to be borne by descendants of Fu Xi (伏羲), a legendary figure prior to the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ). (ii) said to be traced back to Mi Kang Gong (密康公, also written as 宓康公, Duke Kang of Mi, died 922  BC ), ruler of the state of Mi during the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ).

variants.name: Mai
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Thanh, Hoa, Son, Dung, Long, Binh, Minh, Thu, Hue, Huong, Thuy.  Chinese Chi, Hong, Ho, Sang, Han, Hang, Jian, Li, Man, Mei, Sen, Wei.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 麥, meaning ‘wheat’ in Chinese: (i) from Mai Qiu (麥丘), the title of a man in the state of Qi (located mainly in present-day Shandong province) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), who, despite being very old, came to celebrate the birthday of the Duke Huan of Qi (ruler of the state of Qi, died 643  BC ). His descendants inherited 麥, the first part of his title, as their surname. (ii) borne by descendants of Mai Tie Zhang (麥鐵杖), a famous general during the Sui dynasty (581–618  AD ). His descendants were scattered around southern China.

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 買, meaning ‘buy’ in Chinese: (i) borne by descendants of the king Song Weizi, founder of the state of Song. (ii) borne by descendants of the Duke Dao of Xu (ruler of the state of Xu, 546–523  BC ), possibly inheriting his personal name. (iii) from the first element of the personal name Mai Zhu Chu (買朱鉏), style name of an official who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iv) it has also been traced back to other ethnic groups in northern China.

variants.name: Bi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Weihua, Cheng, Hui, Keping, Kun, Lei, Li Ping, Ming, Qi, Tao, Weizhen, Wenyi.  Vietnamese Chau, Hai, Hao, Hung, Mui, Nguyen, Nhung, Tran, Tuan.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 畢, originally denoting a kind of hand-net used in hunting or meaning ‘to complete’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Bi (畢), the name of a fief (located in present-day Shaanxi province), which was granted to Bi Gong Gao (畢公高, or Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (1152–1056  BC ). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chu Lian (出連) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 秘 (also pronounced as Bei or Mi in Mandarin in some local regions), meaning ‘secret’ in Chinese: surname traced back to some powerful clans in the Qiang minority ethnic group in ancient western China.

variants.name: Lua
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Francisco, Jesus, Juan, Dalia, Fernando, Ignacio, Jose Luis, Josefina, Luis, Manuel, Mario.

Galician (Lúa): habitational name from a place called Lúa in Lugo province (Galicia, Spain), from the name of a river, of pre-Roman origin. This surname is apparently no longer found in Galicia (Spain).
Hawaiian: unexplained.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William DE NEVILLE MP was born about 1336, in Rolleston, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Thomas de Neville, was 37 and his mother, Cecily Blanchminster, was 33. He married Elizabeth Fencotte in 1359, in Rolleston, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died about 1420, in his hometown, at the age of 86.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William DE NEVILLE MP est né environ 1336, à Rolleston, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Thomas de Neville, avait 37 ans et sa mère, Cecily Blanchminster, avait 33 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Fencotte en 1359, à Rolleston, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé environ 1420, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 86 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als William DE NEVILLE MP um 1336 in Rolleston, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas de Neville 37 und seine Mutter Cecily Blanchminster 33. Er hat 1359 in Rolleston, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom Elizabeth Fencotte geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb um 1420 im Alter von 86 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William DE NEVILLE MP è nato il circa 1336 nel Rolleston, Nottinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Thomas de Neville, aveva 37 anni e il suo madre, Cecily Blanchminster, aveva 33 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth Fencotte nel 1359, nel Rolleston, Nottinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì circa 1420, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 86 anni.
